title: Oryctanthus guatemalensis
taxonomy: Oryctanthus guatemalensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. This family consists of about 73 genera and 2,000 species of hemiparasitic plants. Oryctanthus guatemalensis is native to Central America. It is a type of mistletoe, which obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The classification of Oryctanthus guatemalensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the order Santalales.

intro: Oryctanthus guatemalensis is a shrub-like plant native to Central America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oryctanthus guatemalensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, Oryctanthus guatemalensis is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are the most common herbal preparations made from this plant.
